target: 5. Like an Idol
Phew…
(I've calmed down a bit...)
Shin-chan, can I come in?
Yeah, it's fine.

Already ready?
You're quick.
I'm in the swim club, after all. I'm used to changing clothes. Did you have enough towels?
Yeah, there were some left in the closet.
Wouldn't towels that have been left for two years smell bad?
Haha... well, just a little.
You're fine, Shin-chan, I left a towel and some dried clothes in the changing room...
Once Kaori-chan finishes her shower and gets ready, we should be able to leave right away.
Yeah, that's right.
By the way... I kind of forced her to come to our place, but I wonder if Kaori-chan found it annoying?
Please come to my place!
Huh?
I can't let you go to the inn completely soaked.
U-uh, it's okay. I'll dry on the way.
It's my fault for messing up on my bike in the first place! Please, just take a shower at our place.
But today's your sister's…
Ah… you heard, huh?
It's fine. If I let you go back alone from here, Onee-chan would definitely get mad at me.
Ah…
Either way, Shin-chan and I have to go home first too, so how about you come with us, Koyuba-san?
Yeah… I'll take you up on that.
Now that it's decided, let's hurry, Koyuba-san.
Oh, right, Kofune-san. You can just call me Kaori.
Uh… Kaori-chan?
Calling her "chan" all of a sudden is rude.
R-Right.

No, it's okay.
It's easier to talk like that, and it feels nice.
Then call me Mio-chan too.
Mio-chan… yeah, I got it.

Well, it's fine.
She said she was grateful too.
I think she was just being polite when she refused so much at first.
…Well, that's fine then.
Even though you haven't seen her in a while, you sure seem to understand Kaori-chan pretty well.
Huh… what?

Huh?
Nothing at all.
(Still... I meant to save Mio, but ended up being saved instead. I feel bad for both Mio and Koyuba-san.)
(But maybe it's a good thing I got to spend some time with Koyuba-san like this.)
(Earlier I warned her not to walk around alone at night, but...)
(Maybe I can get a little more information, like why she was walking alone in the first place.)
Shin-chan, what are you thinking about? Could it be about Kaori-chan?
Huh? How did you know?
Whoa, what a pervert.
What? Why?
Because Kaori-chan is taking a shower right now, you know? What kind of things were you imagining?
I-I wasn't thinking about anything like that!
Shin-chan, don't tell me... you two didn't come back from Tokyo together, did you?
What do you mean?
Like, you didn't actually just reunite earlier, but you were already close back in Tokyo...
Why would you think that? Us being on the same boat was just a coincidence.
Then why did Kaori-chan know that Shin-chan couldn't swim?
Huh…?
After Shin-chan fell into the sea, Kaori-chan said something.
I thought Ajiro-kun had learned how to swim.'
Now that you mention it, she did say that…
I don't think someone you were only with in elementary school would remember whether you could swim or not.
Well… that's true…
See! You two know each other!
It's a misunderstanding!
But it's just so weird. Shin-chan, you were supposed to be reuniting after a long time, but you didn't seem nostalgic or anything at all.
(Ugh... well, I've already experienced reuniting with her once before...)
Plus that face… I feel like I've seen it somewhere…
Huh?
Mio-cha~n, thanks for the shower.
Huh? Mio-chan?
Over here!
Ah, there you are. Ajiro-kun too.
Ah…
(Huh…)
(Koyuba-san... I thought so when we first met too, but)
(Is it because she just finished getting ready? She looks even more beautiful...)

Oh! Right!
Are your clothes okay?
I hope they dried properly.

Yeah, they're fine.
Thanks for the towel and hairdryer.
I feel refreshed~

That's good.
I'm relieved.
Hey, Shin-chan...! Why is your face red!?
Well, you're turning red too, Mio.
B-Because... looking at her again, she's really cute, or rather...
Like an idol…
…An idol?
Wh-What's wrong, Mio-chan? Is there something on my face?
Ah! Oh no!!!
Huh?!
What's wrong, Mio? Don't just shout all of a sudden.
W-Wait right there!!
(What's with her…?)
W-what's going on?

I don't know.
She went to her room, I think.
Well, she'll probably be back soon, so just relax.
U-Um, could this be... Ajiro-kun's room?
Yeah.
U-uh! Uhh!
Wh-What's wrong? Your face suddenly got stiff.
It's nothing.
It doesn't look like nothing... Your face is red, too.
Maybe you caught a cold?
A-ah, no! That's not it!
W-Well, when I realized I was in Ajiro-kun's room, I got kind of nervous.
Don't worry about it.
I moved most of my personal belongings to Tokyo, so it's basically like an empty room now.
No, I don't think... that's true.
Why?
The room has been cleaned up nicely, hasn't it?
I'm sure someone has been taking good care of it because it's Ajiro-kun's place.
I see…

Ah, sorry.
I was staring.
(Ah... this feeling right now, it kind of reminds me of how she used to be.)
(But even though I can remember her personality like this, I have almost no memories of playing together.)
(Since she's never been here before, she and Ushio probably weren't close enough to visit each other's houses either...)

Oh, right.
Thanks for helping me earlier.
N-No, don't worry about it. My body just moved on its own.
You were the same, weren't you, Ajiro-kun? Jumping in to save Mio-chan when she fell into the sea, even though you can't swim.
I can't swim…
Huh…?
About that... You sure remembered that I couldn't swim, huh?
Ah… that's…
Do you remember? A long time ago, when Ajiro-kun was reading a book about underwater ruins, we talked about whether there might be some on Hitogashima too...
When I said, 'Why don't you try diving and looking for them?', Ajiro-kun said, 'I can't swim, so that's impossible.'
I didn't know Ajiro-kun couldn't swim, and I felt like I said something really insensitive... I regretted it a lot.
"I don't remember…"
Now that you mention it...'
Did that really happen? I don't remember...
I see...
But I don't think it was an insensitive comment at all. The fact that I didn't remember means I probably didn't mind it back then either.

Thank you.
I feel a bit relieved.
Now that you mention it...
…Did you remember?
Yeah... right, that's it! And then I said, 'If I go, I'd probably need a submarine or something.'
Eh…?

Wait, was that wrong?
I thought I said that.
W-Was that how it went...?
Probably, at that time, I was so caught up in the fact that I said something insensitive that it was all I could think about...
Ah, so that's why you don't remember.
Y-Yeah... probably.
You really didn't need to worry about it so much. I didn't think it was an insensitive comment back then, and I certainly don't think so now.
I-I see... You're as kind as ever.
(Oh, I see, that's what it was about.)
(And yet Mio went and had some weird misunderstanding...)

I finally found it!
This one, this one!

A magazine?
What's so special about that?
Ah... w-wait a minute, Mio-chan!
This! Look, Kaori-chan!!
Eh…?
Ah…
Eeeeh!?
Wh-Why is Koyuba-san on the cover of a magazine!?
This is you, right, Kaori-chan? It's not just a look-alike, right!?
Umm... well...
Yes, it's definitely me.
<color=#GF_TIPS_122>I knew it, it's the idol Kaori Koyuba-chan!</color> I kept thinking I'd 'seen her somewhere before'!
Y-You're a celebrity!?
What's with you, Shin-chan. You didn't realize at all?
You didn't realize right away either, did you?
Well, I'm not really familiar with idols.
I'm the same.
Ah...! Maybe it would have been better if I pretended not to know!?
No, I'm honestly happy that you noticed.
It's just... if I talk about my job, there are a lot of things I'd have to explain, so I thought...
Besides, I still have to keep it a secret that I'm currently on this island.
(Come to think of it, Koyuba-san mentioned that the town hall prepared her accommodation.)
So you came back to the island because of your idol work?
Yeah, something like that.
You shouldn't ask things like that, Shin-chan. She has a duty of confidentiality, you know.
Where did you even learn that word?
Hey, hey, Kaori-chan. Could you try doing something idol-like for us?
Eh!?! Something idol-like!?
You really don't hold back, do you...
Well, it's not every day you get the chance to talk to an idol.
U-Umm...
Koyuba-san, you don't have to force yourself to go along with her unreasonable requests.
No, it's okay. I'll do my best!
(So this is what they call professionalism...)
...Ahem. Alright, here I go.
Ringing right into your heart. I'm Kaori Koyuba, also known as Kaorin.
This time, I've come to Hitogashima's summer festival to perform a secret solo live show~~~~.
...Something like that.
Whoa~...! She's a real live idol~! Right, Shin-chan?
Y-Yeah...
Uu… I'm so embarrassed.
